metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2111
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.3B/C Rich2 KD (shRNA) decreases transferrin-rhodamine recycling in neurons and COS-7 cells [fluorescent pulse-chase].

Fig.3D Rich2 promotes recycling of GFP-tagged AMPARs to the surface of hipp neurons

Fig.4/5 Rich2 activity is needed for spine enlargement induced by chemical LTP and increase of synaptic currents, and increase of surface levels of synaptic AMPARs [fixed tissue] (NMDAR activation) [Rich2 KD, OE Rich2 mutant proteins]

11/12/2017 Pim
- Since ARHGAP44 / Rich2 is a regulator of signaling and not a transporting protein itself I have selected the "regulates..." variant of the term.. Experimental description: "Neuronal hippocampal cultures were prepared from embryos of either sex of E-17 Swiss mice and grown in Neurobasal medium supplemented with B27 and 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS)."
